narrative: Growing up was traumatic—there was a lot of chaos at home and no real stability. Ever since then, I notice I struggle to stay steady; I've been depressed. That instability seems to follow me, and now the pressure has been relentless and it never lets up with my bills and my job. When this kind of constant stress hits, I find myself pulling away from everyone and isolating myself, just cutting people off entirely. I’ve also noticed that when the pressure builds up, my emotions are completely all over the place and I can't seem to level them out. It feels like those old patterns from my childhood leave me with this same inability to control my emotions when it gets bad. I've been trying to eat better lately, but regardless, I just feel overwhelmed by everything.

Looking back, growing up was traumatic—there was a lot of chaos at home. Ever since then, I notice that I’ve been under constant stress; the pressure from my job and bills just never lets up. When the stress peaks, I find that my emotions are completely all over the place and I can't control them. This instability often leads to me pulling away from everyone and isolating myself. I've also noticed that whenever I can't pull myself together emotionally, or when I'm alone because I've cut people off, I can't stop going over the same thoughts again and again. My mind just repeats the same failures in an endless loop. All of this combined leaves me feeling empty; I am depressed. I did start a new exercise routine last month, but that doesn't change the way my head works. Because of that history of instability in my youth, I've always struggled to stay level, and it feels like it just feeds into everything else I'm dealing with now.

I’ve been struggling so much lately because I break down and can't pull myself together emotionally whenever something goes wrong. These episodes tend to leave me in a state where I keep replaying everything endlessly and can't shut it off, just looping through the same failures and regrets for hours on end. Once those cycles start, it feels impossible to break free from the mental loop. Because of this pattern, I feel depressed. I just don't have the energy to fight it anymore. I moved to a new place recently, but regardless of where I am, these patterns follow me. It starts with that initial loss of control, then the endless repetition in my head, and finally this heavy sense of hopelessness that takes over everything else. I just want to find a way to stop the cycle before it spirals this far.

I’ve been under constant stress—work, money, all of it—and the pressure has been relentless and it never lets up. It’s this heavy, grinding feeling that persists from the moment I wake up until I finally close my eyes at night. I feel it in my shoulders and my chest, just this permanent state of tension that doesn't fade regardless of the day of the week. It's become the baseline of my entire existence, and I can't seem to find a way to step away from it or catch my breath. On other notes, I got a promotion at work recently and I also started a new hobby. But despite those things, this sense of being overwhelmed by the demands of my life is always there, humming in the background and draining my energy. I just feel like I am constantly braced for the next crisis, waiting for the other shoe to drop while trying to manage everything on my plate.
